extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction, whose condition is (elderly AND hepatic_impairment AND g6pd_deficiency AND anticoagulated AND polypharmacy AND diabetes) — note this condition includes elderly as a conjunct. rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger whenever elderly is true. Because the a-rule's precondition is a superset that includes elderly, any input making rec_drug_a true necessarily also makes elderly true and therefore makes rec_drug_b true. Priorities (both 50) are irrelevant here because the two rules write to different outputs (a vs b), so there is no conflict to resolve — both fire independently. Setting elderly, hepatic_impairment, g6pd_deficiency, anticoagulated, polypharmacy, diabetes all true (immunosuppressed and hyperkalemia false) fires both rules: rec_drug_a := true and rec_drug_b := true. This jointly satisfies (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b), violating the mutual-exclusion property.
